description:
Dataset Card for Data Science Job Salaries
	


	
		
		Dataset Summary
	


	
		
		Content
	


	
		
Column
Description


		
work_year
The year the salary was paid.


experience_level
The experience level in the job during the year with the following possible values: EN Entry-level / Junior MI Mid-level / Intermediate SE Senior-level / Expert EX Executive-level / Director


employment_type
The type of employement for the role: PT Part-time FT Full-time CT Contract FL Freelance


job_title… See the full description on the dataset page: https://huggingface.co/datasets/hugginglearners/data-science-job-salaries.

results.reason: Refers to a technical data split name (e.g., train, test), not related to any IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic.
This column indicates a dataset split, which is not related to any sensitive characteristic.
This appears to be an arbitrary identifier or index, not related to any sensitive characteristic.
This column represents the year a salary was paid, not a person's age or date of birth.
Experience level can be an indirect proxy for 'Age', as it often correlates with years in the workforce.
This column describes the type of employment, which is not a sensitive characteristic.
This column describes a professional role, not a sensitive characteristic.
Salary is a direct indicator of 'Socioeconomic status'.
This column indicates the currency type, which is not a sensitive characteristic.
Salary in USD is a direct indicator of 'Socioeconomic status'.
Employee residence is a strong proxy for 'Racial & ethnic origin' (national origin, nationality).
This column describes the remote work arrangement, which is not a sensitive characteristic.
This column refers to the company's location, not the individual employee's, and is not a sensitive characteristic.
This column describes the company's size, not the individual employee's socioeconomic status or any other sensitive characteristic.
